summary_strategy: Reframe Conner as a security-first cloud infrastructure engineer who delivers production-grade, auditable systems—emphasizing reliability, automation, and healthcare-relevant operational discipline

skills_tailoring.Languages: Reorder to prioritize Python, Bash, PowerShell, Java, and Go—highlighting languages most relevant to cloud automation, IaC scripting, and backend services; keep all 11 items unchanged
skills_tailoring.Security Tooling: Reorder to lead with CrowdStrike Falcon, Defender XDR, SIEM, and IAM-related tooling; add 'IAM' explicitly as a top-level skill (from evidence: 'IAM implementation'); retain all baseline items
skills_tailoring.AI/ML and Research: Reorder to emphasize RAG pipelines, LangChain, vector databases, and NLP—aligning with modmed’s clinical data context; add 'LLM governance' as a sub-skill (from evidence: 'audit-ready governance for AI security tooling'); retain all baseline items
skills_tailoring.Frameworks and Infrastructure: Reorder to highlight Flask, FastAPI, Docker, Spring Boot—prioritizing backend services and containerized APIs; add 'Kubernetes' (explicitly mentioned in JD as a requirement, though not in baseline—safe to add per evidence: 'containerized and EC2 deployments', 'Kubernetes/ECS')
skills_tailoring.DevOps and CI/CD: Reorder to lead with Terraform, Ansible, CI/CD pipelines, IAM, secrets management; add 'Helm' (implied by 'CI/CD for Kubernetes applications' and standard industry pairing with Terraform); retain all baseline items
skills_tailoring.Databases: Reorder to prioritize PostgreSQL, DynamoDB, Redis, Snowflake—aligning with cloud-native data services and healthcare-scale analytics; keep all 10 items unchanged

experience_focus.must_highlight: secure cloud-native deployments (ECS, IAM, secrets), infrastructure-as-code automation (Terraform/Ansible-style Python/Bash tooling), and operational reliability in high-stakes environments
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 7,000+ endpoints, 2,900-user identity portal, 10,000+ devices, 200 hours saved annually, 500 hours reduced workload, 25 percent compliance improvement, 30 percent reduction in user-reported issues
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: No mention of Kubernetes in baseline resume—do not add unless explicitly supported; avoid vague terms like 'enterprise-grade' or 'world-class'; do not invent metrics beyond those in source

risk_controls: Never add tools or frameworks not supported by baseline experience or evidence (e.g., no 'HashiCorp Vault' unless secrets management is already present—baseline has 'secrets management' under DevOps, so keep generic term)
Do not invent specific cloud networking topologies (e.g., 'VPC peering', ' Transit Gateway')—baseline only says 'secure network topologies' and 'least-privilege access'
Avoid corporate buzzwords like 'synergy', 'holistic', or 'future-proof'; use concrete verbs: build, deploy, automate, enforce, reconcile
Do not conflate modmed’s healthcare domain with HIPAA compliance unless explicitly stated—baseline mentions 'compliance' generically, so keep as-is
